Skilled Sapiens is an ISO-certified consulting and experiential learning platform committed to delivering industry-relevant Leadership Programs, Live Projects, Career Mentorship, Professional Certifications, and Business Consulting services. As industries continue to evolve with changing technologies, workforce expectations, and emerging skill requirements, it is essential that the organization's learning solutions remain aligned with market demand.

Developing successful educational programs requires more than subject expertise—it demands a deep understanding of industry trends, learner expectations, competitor offerings, pricing strategies, and changing career aspirations. Data-driven market research enables organizations to make informed decisions regarding program design, customer experience, marketing, and long-term business growth.

This project focuses on conducting structured market research and strategic data analysis to identify opportunities for enhancing Skilled Sapiens' program portfolio. Students will work as Business Analysts and Market Research Consultants, collecting secondary research, analyzing industry data, benchmarking competitors, and developing strategic recommendations that support future program development and business expansion.

The project emphasizes research, analysis, and strategic thinking rather than implementation, enabling students to apply consulting methodologies to solve real business problems.

","sectionType":"introduction","title":"Introduction"},{"description":"

Students are encouraged to explore topics such as EdTech and professional learning trends, market research methodologies, competitor benchmarking, customer persona development, pricing strategies, learning experience design, skill gap analysis, business intelligence, strategic decision-making, and emerging technologies influencing the future of education and consulting.

","sectionType":"custom","title":"Suggested Research Areas"},{"description":"

Upon successful completion of this project, students will gain practical experience in conducting structured market research, interpreting business data, and transforming insights into strategic recommendations. They will learn how to evaluate industry trends, benchmark competitors, understand customer expectations, and support product and business strategy through evidence-based decision-making. The project will also help students strengthen their analytical thinking, consulting approach, business communication, and presentation skills while providing exposure to real-world market research assignments commonly performed by business analysts, strategy consultants, and product teams.

","sectionType":"custom","title":"Expected Learning Outcomes"}]
projects.brief:
As a Business Analyst and Market Research Consultant, you have been assigned the responsibility of helping Skilled Sapiens make informed strategic decisions through structured market research and data analysis. Your role is to study the external market environment, understand customer behavior, evaluate competitor strategies, and identify opportunities that can support the organization's future growth.

Rather than designing or implementing new programs, your focus will be on generating meaningful insights that guide strategic decision-making. You will apply research methodologies, business analysis frameworks, and data interpretation techniques to understand changing industry dynamics and recommend practical improvements across product development, pricing, customer experience, and market positioning.

The final outcome of the project should provide Skilled Sapiens with a research-driven perspective on how its programs can evolve to meet future market needs while strengthening its competitive position in the experiential learning and consulting ecosystem.
projects.deliverables:
[{"format":"Rich text","note":"

The primary objective of this project is to develop a research-backed understanding of the evolving education, consulting, and professional learning ecosystem to support the strategic growth of Skilled Sapiens. Students are expected to evaluate current market dynamics, identify emerging opportunities, understand customer expectations, and analyze competitor offerings to recommend improvements across program development, pricing, and market positioning.

Through this project, students will explore how data and market intelligence can be translated into actionable business recommendations that enable organizations to remain customer-centric, future-ready, and competitive.

Project Deliverables:
 
Market Research Report

Students will prepare a comprehensive market research report that provides an overview of the education, consulting, and professional upskilling industry. The report should identify emerging trends, high-demand skills, changing learner preferences, and evolving market opportunities. It should also examine the major factors influencing customer decision-making and highlight potential opportunities for Skilled Sapiens to strengthen its market position.

Customer Persona Framework

Students will develop detailed customer personas representing the primary audience segments served by Skilled Sapiens, including students, working professionals, entrepreneurs, and career switchers. Each persona should capture demographic characteristics, career aspirations, learning goals, challenges, motivations, preferred learning formats, and factors influencing purchasing decisions. These personas should provide a structured understanding of customer needs and support future product and marketing decisions.

Competitive Benchmark Analysis

Students will conduct a detailed comparative analysis of organizations operating in similar domains, including EdTech companies, consulting firms, and professional learning platforms. The analysis should compare competitors across parameters such as program offerings, pricing strategies, delivery models, mentorship support, certifications, marketing approaches, and customer engagement practices. Based on these findings, students should identify areas where Skilled Sapiens can differentiate itself and strengthen its competitive advantage.

Pricing and Learning Delivery Analysis

Students will research prevailing pricing models and learning delivery approaches adopted across the industry. This analysis should evaluate different pricing strategies, program durations, mentorship formats, live and self-paced learning models, certification structures, and technology-enabled learning experiences. Based on market findings, students should recommend suitable pricing and delivery approaches that align with the expectations of Skilled Sapiens' target audience.

Strategic Recommendation Report

Based on the research findings, students will prepare a strategic recommendation report highlighting key insights, opportunities, and actionable recommendations for Skilled Sapiens. The report should provide suggestions for future program development, product positioning, pricing strategy, learner engagement, and business expansion. Every recommendation should be supported by market research, industry trends, and customer insights.

Executive Summary Presentation

Students will prepare a concise executive presentation summarizing the research methodology, major findings, customer insights, competitor analysis, strategic recommendations, and expected business impact. The presentation should be designed for senior management discussions and communicate the most important insights in a structured and professional manner.

","title":"Project Deliverables"}]

projects.brief:

However, a large percentage of prospective learners interact with the brand only once, through a webinar, website visit, social media campaign, or resource download—and fail to continue their engagement or enroll in programs.

As a Marketing Consultant, you have been tasked with designing a comprehensive email marketing strategy that nurtures learners from their first interaction with Skilled Sapiens until they become active learners, program alumni, and brand advocates.

Your strategy should ensure that Skilled Sapiens remains the first platform students think of when considering career development, leadership training, live projects, internships, or placement preparation.

You are expected to leverage customer segmentation, personalized communication, automation workflows, compelling content, and data-driven optimization techniques to create an email marketing framework that enhances learner engagement, improves brand recall, and drives measurable business outcomes.

[{"description":"

The Certification in Equity Valuation & Financial Modeling Live Project is designed to provide participants with practical exposure to the end-to-end equity research and valuation process followed by investment banks, equity research firms, private equity firms, consulting organizations, and institutional investors.

Throughout this project, participants will work on a real listed company and apply industry-standard financial analysis techniques to understand its business model, competitive positioning, financial performance, and intrinsic value. The project emphasizes a research-driven approach, requiring participants to collect information from authentic sources, build dynamic financial models, perform valuation, and formulate an evidence-based investment recommendation.

By successfully completing this project, participants will develop the analytical, technical, and decision-making skills required for careers in Equity Research, Investment Banking, Corporate Finance, Asset Management, and Financial Consulting.

","sectionType":"introduction","title":"Introduction"}]
projects.brief:
As part of this live project, participants will undertake a comprehensive equity research and valuation assignment on Dabur India Ltd. using only publicly available information, with the company's official Annual Reports serving as the primary source for historical financial data.

Participants will begin by analysing the company's historical performance, management commentary, and industry environment before manually compiling five years of consolidated financial statements into a dynamic Excel-based financial model. Based on their research and financial analysis, they will prepare forecasts, estimate the company's intrinsic value using appropriate valuation techniques, compare the company with its peers through relative valuation, and evaluate the key drivers influencing business performance and valuation.

The project concludes with the preparation of a professional investment research report summarizing the participant's analysis, observations, valuation findings, risks, and final investment recommendation. Together, the Excel financial model and supporting report will demonstrate the participant's ability to conduct a structured equity research assignment comparable to those performed in professional finance and investment roles.
projects.deliverables:
[{"format":"Rich text","note":"Live Project Scope of Work in Equity Valuation & Financial Modeling:


As part of this live project, you are required to conduct a comprehensive equity research and valuation exercise on Dabur India Ltd. The objective of this project is to help you develop practical skills in financial statement analysis, industry research, financial modeling, valuation, and investment recommendation.

Project Deliverables:

1. Annual Report Collection

  • Download the latest five years' Annual Reports of Dabur India Ltd.

  • Only the official Annual Reports published by the company should be used throughout this project.

2. Management Discussion & Analysis (MD&A)

  • Carefully review the Management Discussion & Analysis (MD&A) section of each of the last five Annual Reports.

  • Summarize the key management insights, strategic initiatives, industry trends, risks, opportunities, and future outlook highlighted over the five-year period.

  • Prepare concise observations on how the company's strategy and business environment have evolved.

3. Historical Financial Statement Compilation

  • Extract the Consolidated Financial Statements for the last five financial years directly from the Annual Reports.

  • Manually populate all historical financial data into your Excel model.

  • Important: No third-party financial databases or websites are permitted.

The use of platforms such as:

  • Moneycontrol

  • Screener

  • CMIE

  • ACE Equity

  • Tickertape

  • Trendlyne

  • Any other financial database will not be accepted. Every figure must be sourced directly from the company's published Annual Reports.

4. Industry Analysis

Conduct a detailed analysis of the industry in which Dabur operates. Your analysis should include, but is not limited to, the following:

  • Apply Porter's Five Forces Framework and evaluate the competitive dynamics of the industry.

  • Estimate the current size of the industry and its expected growth potential.

  • Identify the major competitors and estimate their respective market shares.

  • Explain the business models of leading companies in the industry.

  • Compare operating margins and profitability across major players.

  • Assess the overall market opportunity in India.

  • Identify and explain the major revenue drivers and cost drivers influencing the business.

  • Support your observations with logical reasoning and relevant research.

5. Financial Model Development

Prepare a fully dynamic Three-Statement Financial Model in Microsoft Excel.

The model should:

  • Link the Income Statement, Balance Sheet, and Cash Flow Statement correctly.

  • Maintain complete formula integrity.

  • Automatically update financial statements when assumptions are changed.

  • Follow best practices in financial modeling.

6. Forecast Assumptions

Prepare detailed forecasting assumptions covering all major operating and financial line items.

For each assumption:

  • Clearly explain the rationale.

  • Support assumptions using historical trends, industry outlook, management commentary, or logical reasoning.

7. Valuation Methodology Selection

Determine the appropriate valuation approach.

You should:

  • Decide whether FCFF (Free Cash Flow to Firm) or FCFE (Free Cash Flow to Equity) is more appropriate.

  • Clearly justify your selection based on the company's characteristics and capital structure.

8. Cost of Capital Calculation

Calculate the appropriate discount rate.

Depending on your chosen valuation methodology, compute:

  • Cost of Equity

  • Weighted Average Cost of Capital (WACC)

Include all workings, assumptions, formulas, and sources used in the calculation.

9. Intrinsic Valuation

Perform a complete Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) valuation.

Your valuation should include:

  • Forecast period

  • Terminal value calculation

  • Discounting methodology

  • Enterprise Value

  • Equity Value

  • Intrinsic Value per Share

Clearly explain your methodology and interpretation of the results.

10. Sensitivity Analysis

Perform sensitivity analysis to evaluate the impact of key assumptions on valuation.

Identify the variables that have the greatest influence on intrinsic value, such as:

  • Revenue Growth

  • EBITDA Margin

  • Terminal Growth Rate

  • WACC / Discount Rate

Present the findings using appropriate sensitivity tables or charts.

11. Relative Valuation

Conduct a peer comparison using commonly used valuation multiples, including:

  • Price-to-Earnings (P/E)

  • Price-to-Sales (P/S)

  • EV / EBITDA

Compare Dabur with its listed peers and interpret whether the company appears relatively overvalued, undervalued, or fairly valued.

12. Financial Ratio Analysis

Calculate and analyse relevant financial ratios, including:

  • Profitability Ratios

  • Liquidity Ratios

  • Efficiency Ratios

  • Solvency Ratios

  • Return Ratios

Interpret the trends over the historical period and provide meaningful business insights.

13. Investment Recommendation Report

Prepare a professional report of at least five pages summarizing your complete analysis.

The report should include:

  • Executive Summary

  • Company Overview

  • Industry Analysis

  • Financial Performance Analysis

  • Valuation Summary

  • Key Risks

  • Investment Recommendation (Buy / Hold / Sell)

  • Conclusion

Your report should focus on insights, interpretation, and recommendations rather than reproducing raw financial data.

Final Submission Requirements:

You are required to submit either of the following:

1. Microsoft Excel Financial Model

The Excel workbook should include:

  • Historical Financial Statements

  • Dynamic Three-Statement Model

  • Forecast Assumptions

  • DCF Valuation

  • Sensitivity Analysis

  • Relative Valuation

  • Financial Ratio Analysis

2. Supporting Report (Word/PDF)

The accompanying report should primarily focus on:

  • Observations

  • Analysis

  • Interpretation

  • Business insights

  • Final recommendation

The report should not duplicate the numerical schedules already presented in the Excel model. Instead, it should explain the rationale behind your analysis and highlight the key findings that support your investment recommendation.

","title":"Project Deliverables"}]

In today's rapidly evolving business environment, organizations must make strategic decisions based on reliable market intelligence, customer insights, and competitive analysis. As industries continue to transform due to technological advancements, changing consumer behavior, and increasing competition, businesses can no longer rely solely on intuition or historical experience to guide their growth strategies. Structured Market Research and Analysis (MRA) has therefore become a critical business capability for identifying opportunities, understanding customer needs, evaluating competitors, and developing sustainable competitive advantages.

Skilled Sapiens operates in the experiential learning, leadership development, and consulting ecosystem through its Campus Connect and Business Connect initiatives. As the organization continues to expand its portfolio of leadership programs, live projects, mentorship services, and business consulting offerings, it becomes increasingly important to understand the evolving market landscape and continuously refine its strategic positioning.

This project provides students with an opportunity to work as Market Research Analysts and Strategy Consultants, leveraging structured research methodologies alongside Generative AI tools such as ChatGPT to conduct business research and develop strategic recommendations. Rather than simply collecting information, students will learn how to synthesize research findings, validate assumptions, generate meaningful business insights, and present executive-ready recommendations similar to those delivered by consulting firms.

The project emphasizes analytical thinking, structured problem solving, and strategic storytelling while demonstrating how GenAI can accelerate research and improve the quality of business decision-making.

The project will strengthen participants' analytical thinking, business problem-solving, strategic communication, and research capabilities while preparing them for careers in consulting, market research, business analysis, strategy, product management, and growth functions where data-driven decision-making and AI-assisted workflows are becoming increasingly important.

projects.brief:
As a Market Research Analyst and Strategy Consultant, your responsibility is to help Skilled Sapiens make data-informed business decisions by conducting structured market research and strategic analysis using both conventional research methodologies and Generative AI tools.

Depending on the chosen business problem, you may analyze emerging skill trends, evaluate customer personas, benchmark competitors, identify market opportunities, or design scalable market research frameworks. Throughout the project, your role is not merely to collect information but to interpret research findings, validate assumptions, identify meaningful patterns, and transform fragmented data into actionable business recommendations.

Generative AI should be used as an analytical accelerator that improves research efficiency, supports idea generation, organizes information, and assists in synthesizing complex findings. However, students are expected to exercise independent judgment, validate outputs, and apply business reasoning while developing their final recommendations.

The final outcome should demonstrate a structured consulting approach to solving real business problems while showcasing the ability to combine research methodologies, analytical thinking, and AI-assisted insight generation.

Students will conduct a detailed competitor analysis of organizations operating within the consulting, mentorship, and professional upskilling ecosystem. The analysis should compare competitors across parameters such as market positioning, product offerings, pricing strategies, delivery models, mentorship approach, customer engagement, and overall value proposition. Based on these findings, students should identify competitive gaps, differentiation opportunities, and potential white spaces that Skilled Sapiens can leverage.

Recommendations should be supported by research findings and aligned with the organization's long-term business objectives.

[{"description":"

The Private Equity & Venture Capital Live Project is designed to provide participants with practical exposure to the investment decision-making process followed by Private Equity funds, Venture Capital firms, Growth Equity investors, and Investment Banking professionals.

In this project, participants will assume the role of an Investment Analyst at Caplexus Capital, an India-focused investment firm with dedicated Private Equity and Venture Capital investment platforms. Participants will evaluate a live investment opportunity by applying structured investment analysis, financial modeling, valuation techniques, commercial due diligence, and investment judgment to determine whether the proposed investment meets the firm's return expectations and risk criteria.

The project closely mirrors the work performed by analysts and associates in leading investment firms, enabling participants to develop industry-relevant skills in deal evaluation, investment thesis formulation, financial modeling, transaction analysis, and investment committee presentation.

","sectionType":"introduction","title":"Introduction"}]
projects.brief:
As an Investment Analyst at Caplexus Capital, you will evaluate a real-world style investment opportunity and prepare the same deliverables that would typically be presented to an Investment Committee before an investment decision is made.

Participants are required to choose one of the following investment tracks:

  • Track A – Private Equity: Evaluate a majority buyout opportunity involving a profitable regional diagnostics company by building a Leveraged Buyout (LBO) model, analysing investment returns, identifying value creation opportunities, and preparing an Investment Committee recommendation.

OR

  • Track B – Venture Capital: Evaluate a Series A investment opportunity in a high-growth health-tech startup by analysing the market opportunity, business model, unit economics, capitalization table, ownership dilution, exit scenarios, and investment returns before recommending investment terms.

For the selected track, participants will build a professional Excel model supporting their analysis and prepare a concise Investment Committee memorandum summarizing the investment thesis, commercial assessment, financial analysis, valuation, risks, expected returns, and final recommendation.

The objective is not to arrive at a single "correct" answer, but to demonstrate sound investment judgment, analytical rigor, financial modeling capability, and the ability to communicate investment recommendations in a clear, structured, and professional manner.
projects.deliverables:
[{"format":"Rich text","note":"
Track A – Private Equity:

The MediCore BuyoutProject Scenario

You have joined Caplexus PE, the Private Equity investment arm of Caplexus Capital, as an Investment Analyst. The firm is evaluating the acquisition of MediCore Diagnostics, a profitable regional diagnostics chain operating across Western and Central India.

The investment team believes the company presents an attractive buy-and-build (roll-up) opportunity within a highly fragmented diagnostics market. Your responsibility is to evaluate whether the proposed leveraged buyout generates sufficient returns while identifying the key value creation opportunities and investment risks.

Your Analysis Should Cover1.
Investment Opportunity Assessment

Evaluate whether the proposed acquisition is financially attractive by analysing:

  • Appropriate entry valuation

  • Enterprise Value negotiation range

  • Suitable debt financing structure

  • Optimal leverage (Debt / EBITDA multiple)

  • Acquisition funding mix

2. Leveraged Buyout (LBO) Model

Build a fully functional 5-year Leveraged Buyout (LBO) Model in Microsoft Excel.

Your model should include:

  • Purchase Price Allocation

  • Sources & Uses of Funds

  • Debt Schedule

  • Interest Calculations

  • Mandatory Debt Repayment

  • Free Cash Flow Sweep

  • Operating Forecast

  • Exit Assumptions

  • Equity Value Bridge

The model should be dynamic, formula-driven, and allow assumptions to be changed easily.

3. Investment Return Analysis

Calculate expected investment returns including:

  • Internal Rate of Return (IRR)

  • Multiple on Invested Capital (MOIC)

  • Equity Value at Exit

  • Enterprise Value at Exit

Develop both:

You have joined Caplexus Ventures, the Venture Capital investment arm of Caplexus Capital, as an Investment Analyst.

The firm is evaluating a Series A investment in PathPin, a fast-growing health-tech startup providing at-home diagnostics, AI-powered report interpretation, and teleconsultation services.

Your objective is to evaluate whether PathPin has the characteristics of a venture-scale business capable of generating attractive investment returns.

projects.objectives:
The objective of this live project is to provide participants with hands-on experience in evaluating investment opportunities from the perspective of a Private Equity or Venture Capital investor.

projects.brief:
As an AI Product Manager at Skilled Sapiens, you have been tasked with identifying opportunities where Artificial Intelligence can improve business operations and enhance the learner experience.

Your responsibility is to understand business problems, research AI solutions available in the market, evaluate their suitability, and prioritize initiatives that deliver maximum value. toolkit.content:
General Guidelines for Live Projects

  1. Individual or Group Participation: Students may complete the project individually or form a group of up to four (4) members. Group formation is entirely at the students' discretion; however, all group members must belong to the same college/institution.

  2. Weekly Project Reviews: Students are expected to attend and follow the instructions, guidance, and action items shared by the Project Manager during the weekly live project review sessions.

  3. Self-Paced Learning: This is a self-paced Live Project, and no strict weekly deadlines have been assigned to accommodate students' academic commitments. However, students are strongly encouraged to maintain consistent progress and actively participate throughout the project to maximize their learning experience.

  4. Recommended Time Commitment: Students should dedicate approximately 2–3 hours per week to successfully complete the project and achieve the intended learning outcomes.

  5. Project Report Submission: Upon completion of the project, students must prepare a comprehensive Project Report in either Microsoft Word (.doc/.docx) or PowerPoint (.ppt/.pptx) format for evaluation. There is no prescribed page or slide limit. Students are encouraged to demonstrate originality and creativity by designing their own report structure and presentation format.

  6. Project Completion & Certificate: There is no fixed deadline for submitting the Live Project report. Students may submit their project at any time after completing all the required work. Upon successful evaluation, the Live Project Completion Certificate will generally be issued within 3–4 working days.

  7. Project Selection: Students may choose any one of the available Live Projects. Those enrolled in multiple project roles may either work on all projects simultaneously or complete them sequentially, based on their convenience.

  8. Group Submission Policy: For group projects, all team members must work on a common project report. However, each member is required to submit the report individually through their own LMS account to be eligible for evaluation and certification.

  9. Submission Process: All project submissions will be accepted exclusively through the LMS Portal. Reports submitted via email, WhatsApp, Google Drive links, or any other platform will not be considered for evaluation.
toolkit.summary: Core guidance students should review before working on any live project.
toolkit.title: Live Project Guidelines

toolkit.content:
Week-wise Roadmap for Live Project CompletionImportant Note


The following roadmap is a recommended four-week learning framework designed to help students approach the Live Project in a structured and industry-oriented manner.

Since this is a self-paced project, students who wish to complete the project earlier are welcome to do so. However, they are strongly advised to follow the same sequence of activities (Week 01 → Week 02 & 03 → Week 04) to ensure a systematic learning experience.

Students may accelerate the pace of execution but should not skip or rearrange any stage of the project workflow, as each phase builds the foundation for the next.

Week 01 – Market Research & Company UnderstandingObjective

The objective of Week 1 is to develop a comprehensive understanding of the company, industry, customers, and business environment before beginning the project execution.

1. Project Selection

  • Review the list of available Live Projects under your selected role.

  • Select one project that you wish to work on as your Live Project.

2. Company & Market Research

Conduct detailed research on the company from multiple functional perspectives to develop cross-functional business thinking. This will help you understand how different business functions contribute to solving real-world problems.

Your research should include:

  • Understanding the company's objectives and core offerings

  • Company Analysis

  • Product & Service Analysis

  • Website Analysis

  • Website User Experience (UX) Analysis

  • Customer Segment Analysis

  • Competitor Analysis

3. Business Framework Analysis

Based on your research findings, apply relevant business and strategic frameworks to understand the company's current position and future opportunities.

Suggested frameworks include:

  • SWOT Analysis

  • PESTEL Analysis

  • Ansoff Matrix

  • Any other relevant strategic framework applicable to your project

4. Digital Landscape Research

Evaluate the company's online presence and digital positioning by conducting research on:

  • Official website and digital assets

  • Social media platforms

  • Brand visibility and customer engagement

  • Existing marketing strategies

  • Competitor social media analysis

  • Market gaps and potential opportunities

Week 02 & Week 03 – Project Execution & DeliverablesObjective

The objective of this phase is to solve the assigned business problem by applying your research findings and analytical skills.

Students should:

  • Work on the project objectives and deliverables specified for their selected Live Project.

  • Perform additional market research wherever required.

  • Conduct data analysis and interpret findings.

  • Brainstorm and evaluate multiple solution approaches.

  • Develop practical, research-backed recommendations.

  • Continuously refine the project based on insights generated throughout the execution phase.

This stage should focus on demonstrating:

  • Problem-solving ability

  • Strategic thinking

  • Practical business recommendations

Week 04 – Final Recommendations & Project Report SubmissionObjective

The final week should focus on consolidating all your work into a professional project report.

Students should:

  • Prepare the final project report in either Microsoft Word (.doc/.docx) or PowerPoint (.ppt/.pptx) format.

  • Organize the report in a logical and professional manner.

  • Compile all research findings, analyses, project outcomes, and supporting evidence.

  • Present actionable business recommendations and implementation strategies.

  • Review and proofread the report before submission through the LMS Portal.

The final report should clearly demonstrate:

  • Research methodology

  • Analytical thinking

  • Business understanding

  • Recommendations

  • Overall project outcomes
